data.current_description:
Checks if a document is present in one or more clusters (specific set of field values).

>ℹ️ There is a limit of five rules per request.
data.summary_suggestion: Check document membership in field value clusters
data.description_suggestion: Validates whether a document matches one or more cluster definitions (specific combinations of field values). Accepts up to five cluster rules per request. Returns validation results indicating which clusters the document belongs to. Useful for data segmentation and categorization workflows. Requires appropriate License Manager permissions (Insert or update document, Full access, or Master Data administrator role).
data.reasoning: Original summary is vague about the validation purpose. Description lacks input details (what constitutes a cluster rule) and output details (what validation results are returned). Enhanced with clarity on cluster definitions, request limits, return value, and use case context for better AI agent interpretability.

data.current_description:
Lists the versions of a document.

>ℹ Master Data documents are versioned. This means that, for each change, a new version is generated.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all versions for a specific document. Each version represents a state change of the document. Returns version metadata including version IDs, timestamps, and change information. Supports pagination and filtering parameters. Requires appropriate License Manager permissions (Read only documents, Insert or update document, Full access, or Master Data administrator role).
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description lacks output details (what metadata is returned) and pagination context. Added specificity about pagination support, version metadata returned, and parameter options for better AI agent understanding of how to use this operation.

Updates the document's version value.

data.current_description:
This request allows you to partially update a document corresponding to a given data entity.

>ℹ️ You can use this request to create documents for any given data entity. Because of this, you are not restricted to using the fields exemplified below in your requests. But you should be aware of the fields allowed or required for each document you wish to update.
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new document with an auto-generated ID or partially updates an existing document by merging provided fields with existing data. Only specified fields are modified; omitted fields remain unchanged. Supports any data entity and automatically creates the data entity if it does not exist. Returns the updated or created document.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Create partial document' is misleading as it only mentions partial updates but the operation also creates documents. Description lacks clarity about the dual nature (create vs update), missing information about field merging behavior, and unclear about return values. Enhanced version clarifies both capabilities and explains the merge semantics for AI agents.

data.current_description:
Retrieves Master Data v2 documents' information, while choosing which fields will be returned and filtering documents by specific fields. 

> The response header `REST-Content-Range` indicates the total amount of results for that specific search. For example, it may return `resources 0-100/136108`, which indicates it has returned the first 100 results out of a total of 136108.

Below you can see some query examples and learn more about each query parameter.

Learn more about [Pagination in the Master Data API](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/pagination-in-the-master-data-api). 

>⚠️ Avoid sending too many requests with wildcards (`*`) in the search parameters or that use the `keyword` parameter. This may lead to this endpoint being temporarily blocked for your account. If this happens you will receive an error with status code `503`.
data.summary_suggestion: Search and filter documents with field selection
data.description_suggestion: Searches documents in a data entity using flexible filtering criteria and returns selected fields. Supports complex queries with filters, date ranges, numeric ranges, partial matches, null checks, and comparison operators. Returns paginated results with the `REST-Content-Range` header indicating total count. Use `_fields` parameter to specify returned fields. Avoid excessive wildcard searches to prevent rate limiting (503 errors). Supports sorting and pagination parameters for result navigation.
data.reasoning: Original summary is too generic. Description is verbose and scattered, mixing examples with warnings. Missing clear explanation of return format, pagination mechanism, and how to use key parameters. Enhanced version provides concise summary, clarifies search capabilities, explains pagination via headers, warns about rate limiting, and mentions sorting/pagination parameters for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'Scrolldocuments' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. The summary 'Scroll documents' is too generic and doesn't convey the pagination mechanism or token-based approach. The description, while comprehensive, lacks clarity about the response structure and token handling for AI agents. Improved summary emphasizes the pagination and token mechanism. Enhanced description clarifies the token lifecycle, response structure, and filtering capabilities to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation for large dataset retrieval.

data.current_description:
Returns a list of documents according to query parameter filters. If you need to query the entire database, or your collection is over 10000 documents, use this endpoint.

In the first request, the `X-VTEX-MD-TOKEN` token will be obtained in the response header. This token must be passed to the next request in the `_token` query string parameter. The token expires after 20 minutes of inactivity, and each request made with the token during this time resets the expiration timer.

After the token is obtained, it is no longer necessary to send the filter or document size per page parameters. You only need to resend the token until the document collection is empty. 

First request:
```
/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?isCluster=true&_size=250&_fields=email,firstName 
```

Retrieve the token in the header `X-VTEX-MD-TOKEN` from the first request's response and use it to make the following requests.

Subsequent requests:
```
/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?_token={tokenValue}
 ```

Learn more about [Pagination in the Master Data API](https://developers.vtex.com/docs/guides/pagination-in-the-master-data-api). 

>⚠️ Avoid sending too many requests with wildcards (`*`) in the search parameters or using the `keyword` parameter. This may lead to this endpoint being temporarily blocked for your account. If this happens you will receive an error with status code `429`.

## Query examples

| **Query Type** | **Example** |
|-|-|
| **Simple filter** | `/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?email=my@email.com` |
| **Complex filter** | `/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?_where=(firstName=Jon OR lastName=Smith) OR (createdIn between 2001-01-01 AND 2016-01-01)` |
 | **Date range** | `/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?_where=createdIn between 2001-01-01 AND 2016-01-01` |
| **Range numeric fields** | `/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?_where=age between 18 AND 25` |
| **Partial filter** | `/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?firstName=*Maria*` |
| **Filter for null values** | `/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?_where=firstName is null` |
| **Filter for non-null values** | `/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?_where=firstName is not null` |
| **Filter for difference** | `/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?_where=firstName<>maria` |
| **Filter greater than** | `/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?_where=number>5` |
| **Filter less than** | `/dataentities/Newsletter/scroll?_where=date<2001-01-01` |
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve paginated documents with token-based scrolling
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of documents from a data entity using token-based scrolling for efficient handling of large datasets (over 10,000 documents). Supports filtering via query parameters. Returns a document list and an `X-VTEX-MD-TOKEN` header on the first request; use this token in subsequent requests via the `_token` parameter to continue pagination. The token expires after 20 minutes of inactivity. Supports various filter types including date ranges, numeric ranges, partial matches, and null checks. Note: Excessive wildcard or keyword searches may result in temporary endpoint blocking (HTTP 429).
data.reasoning: The summary 'Scroll documents' is too generic and doesn't convey the pagination mechanism or token-based approach. The description, while comprehensive, lacks clarity about the response structure and token handling for AI agents. Improved summary emphasizes the pagination and token mechanism. Enhanced description clarifies the token lifecycle, response structure, and filtering capabilities to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation for large dataset retrieval.

Enhanced description clarifies the conditional behavior (create if not exists, update if exists), mentions the trigger capability, specifies the return value, and includes the schema limit constraint for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description:
Creates or edits a data entity schema. Learn more about [Master Data schemas](https://developers.vtex.com/vtex-rest-api/docs/master-data-schema-lifecycle). 

> Note that if you send a `schemaName` that does not exist for that data entity, this request will create it.

This request can also be used to [create or edit Master Data v2 triggers](https://developers.vtex.com/vtex-rest-api/docs/setting-up-triggers-in-master-data-v2). 

> Each Master Data v2 data entity can have up to 60 schemas.
data.summary_suggestion: Create or update data entity schema
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new schema or updates an existing schema for a data entity. If the specified `schemaName` does not exist, it will be created; otherwise, the existing schema will be updated. This operation can also be used to create or edit Master Data v2 triggers. Returns the created or updated schema. Note: Each data entity can have a maximum of 60 schemas.
data.reasoning: The summary 'Save schema by name' is vague and doesn't clearly indicate the create-or-update behavior. The description mentions the dual functionality but lacks clarity about the response structure. Improved summary uses 'Create or update' to be more explicit about the operation's dual nature. Enhanced description clarifies the conditional behavior (create if not exists, update if exists), mentions the trigger capability, specifies the return value, and includes the schema limit constraint for better AI agent understanding.
